title: Entrainment
definition: The process by which two or more systems come to vibrate in harmony.

difficulty_levels.beginner: When two things start moving at the same rhythm, like when you tap your foot to a song. It's like dancing without music but in sync with your own beat.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The phenomenon where an external frequency influences a system to resonate at the same frequency, often observed in physics and music.
difficulty_levels.expert: A fundamental principle of systems dynamics, describing how a system will adjust its internal properties to align with an external frequency. This concept is crucial in understanding complex systems such as biological organisms, ecological networks, and cosmic phenomena.

extra_fields.insight: The realization that the practice of chanting can be seen not just as a linguistic exercise, but also as a process of entrainment where one's body tunes itself to resonate with the universal frequency.
